text: Sex, age, BMI, and race-specific training are all independently associated with race velocity across all endurance race distances.
The performance gap between male and female runners decreases as race distance increases.
Tempo runs are more strongly associated with race velocity at shorter distances, while weekly training mileage and interval training have similar associations with velocity across all race distances.

The Riegel formula is well-calibrated for predicting race times up to the half-marathon but dramatically underestimates marathon time, giving times at least 10 minutes too fast for half of recreational runners.
specific_value: ≥10 min too fast for 50% of runners

text: Marathon prediction models using one or two prior race times substantially outperform the Riegel formula, reducing mean squared error from 381 (Riegel) to 228 (one prior race) or 208 (two prior races).
Using two prior race results for marathon prediction provides only a modest improvement over using one prior race result.
